subject: Add PNF simulator execution and cleanup as a BaseStep
commit_message:
Add PNF simulator execution and cleanup as a BaseStep

1. The simulator is the masspnfsim from integration project. Added as a git submodule.
2. The flow: build image, bootstrap simulator, run simulator, stop simulator,
remove simulator, remove image.
3. Additionally trigger (sending a message to VES) is handled in pnf/utils.py. To send a request to VES,
vesip in settings.PNF_VES_CONFIG should be set to the one accessible from simulator container. Default one
(172.17.0.1) communicates with the machine the simulator runs at.

Issue-ID: TEST-278
Signed-off-by: Eli Halych <illia.halych@t-mobile.pl>
Change-Id: Ia576881bb53a88364608b0e0bac14d10583e40d4

Patch Set 3:

simulator_source is an external simulator taken from https://github.com/onap/integration/tree/master/test/mocks/mass-pnf-sim and it is in its original form mostly with a few changes to make use of. To execute pnf_run.py where the step is located, Docker 1.19 and JDK8 are needed.

Patch Set 3:

maybe a stupid question...

as the sim is launched as a docker and you mention that most part of the sim remain unchanged, would it be not better to avoid duplicating the code, improve/adapt it directly in the original repo, then build a docker image directly usable by the test from the original repo published on the nexus

I do not know if we could think of a wrapper at pythonsdk-test level to be able to launch any kind of simulator as a docker aside of the tests.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> maybe a stupid question...
> 
> as the sim is launched as a docker and you mention that most part of the sim remain unchanged, would it be not better to avoid duplicating the code, improve/adapt it directly in the original repo, then build a docker image directly usable by the test from the original repo published on the nexus
> 
> I do not know if we could think of a wrapper at pythonsdk-test level to be able to launch any kind of simulator as a docker aside of the tests.

That's a great question Morgan! Avoiding duplicate code would be good. The reason it was added as simulator_source/ folder is that the pre-built image weighted 107MB and it of course made no sense to upload it to this repo. The simulator_source/ however weighted 1MB and could be built in place quite fast and that's why it's in this commit. After that I was looking for the pre-built image in Nexus and haven't found any with the same name (mass-pnf-sim).

We could either (1) pre-build it, upload it to Nexus to have a stable version there and then only pull the image for the our purposes or alternatively (2) we could pull the relevant repo during execution and build it in place. Is the option #1 what you meant? 

The wrapper of course could do it! We'd then need to think about what the starting point for the wrapper would be (like pulling from Nexus, for example). I think for the wrapper then it'd need to be quite universal to support a variety of possible simulators (for example, pull/run/remove). But then again: different simulators would need custom bootstrap and other custom functionalities (like, trigger event register to VES). How do you see such a wrapper?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I can see that this change in most part is a copy-paste of the mass-pnf-sim from integration repo, if you'd like to contribute to that it should go to there I think. Duplicating code like this can be a maintainers mess. 

Usually when there's a need for a code from different repo one adds git submodule, not copy-paste code from elsewhere.

yep the docker is built and is available in 
https://nexus3.onap.org/#browse/search=keyword%3Dpnf:22b3686a9064fa3d74e7b526e6ebe900

I just wonder if it is the right one..but Bartek will confirm

So what I imagine
sim code => in sim repo (no duplication)
in pythonsdk-tests => some code to start any simulator (but let's start with the pnf-sim)
these simulator shall be available as a docker in the nexus or upstream

run_simu(docker_ref, param)

+2 or:

include simu code as a submodule and since for running it I have written the mass-pnf-sim lifecycle manager in Python as MassPnfSim class so it would be possible to import it in sdk directly and then just invoke it just like mass-pnf-sim.py script does it. You could also have a look at test_lifecycle.py for even more explicit guidance.

Can we upload the image for 'masspnf-simulator'? And I don't have permissions to anything in Nexus yet.
